Use this skill when writing or reviewing code for quality and maintainability.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Prefer the simplest solution that works; optimize for readability over cleverness.
- Small, focused units: functions do one thing; files stay cohesive (split before they sprawl).
- Favor immutability and pure functions; isolate side effects.
- Name things for intent; avoid magic numbers; delete dead code rather than commenting it out.
- High cohesion, low coupling; depend on interfaces, not concretions.

## Pull current docs first
Each language/framework has idioms + an official style guide and linter — follow theirs:
- your language's official style guide + the project's formatter/linter config
- Use the project's formatter + linter config as the source of truth; don't weaken it to pass.

## Checklist
- [ ] Functions small + single-purpose; files cohesive
- [ ] Clear names; no magic numbers; no dead/commented-out code
- [ ] Side effects isolated; immutable data preferred
- [ ] Matches the project's formatter/linter (config unchanged to "pass")
- [ ] Public surface documented where non-obvious
